content:
`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
- The image has baked-in copy ("PURE GINGER. Unbottled." + yellow tagline) plus the bottle + ingredient still life. Two ways to put new copy on it without breaking the no-text-on-product rule: (1) replace the image with a version that has the baked copy removed, or (2) keep the image and place new copy in a top or bottom white safe band, treating the baked copy as part of the visual.
- Option 2 is faster but risks copy-on-copy collision with the baked "PURE GINGER. Unbottled." line. Option 1 needs a clean version of the image.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

<question-form id="variant-c-copy" title="Variant C — new copy">
{
  "description": "Two quick decisions and I'll build it.",
  "questions": [
    {
      "id": "approach",
      "label": "How should the new copy live with the image?",
      "type": "radio",
      "required": true,
      "options": [
        "Use image as-is, new copy in a top white safe band (above the baked headline)",
        "Use image as-is, new copy in a bottom white safe band (below the bottle base)",
        "I'll provide a clean version of the image without baked copy",
        "Overlay on whitespace edges — accept some proximity to baked copy"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "copy",
      "label": "What copy? (headline / sub / CTA — or leave blank for me to propose 2 options matching a psych lever)",
      "type": "textarea",
      "placeholder": "e.g. Headline: Summer, uncorked. — Sub: Ginger, lemon, zero compromise. — CTA: Taste it"
    },
    {
      "id": "placements",
      "label": "Which placements?",
      "type": "checkbox",
      "required": true,
      "maxSelections": 4,
      "options": ["Feed 1:1", "Feed 4:5", "Stories 9:16", "Carousel"]
    }
  ]
}
</question-form>
